summary: Iteration 5 retest — Dashboard STILL CRASHES on mount. The compute block was inserted at lines 47-73 of /app/frontend/app/(tabs)/index.tsx, but the underlying state hooks and API calls for `budgets`, `settings`, and `streaks` were NEVER added. As a result, the very first compute line (`const mBud = budgets?.month;`) throws `ReferenceError: budgets is not defined`. Same blocker as iteration 4, just a different identifier in the error message. Frontend testing of streak cards / warning banner / discipline-mode visuals is impossible until this is fixed. Backend not re-run (already 17/17 in iter4).

ui_bugs.issue: Uncaught ReferenceError: budgets is not defined at index.tsx:48:16. The compute block (lines 47-73) references `budgets`, `settings`, and `streaks` but the component never declares them as state, never imports them, and never fetches them in `load()`. JSX further references `streaks?.log_streak`, `streaks?.budget_streak`, `streaks?.budget_streak_possible` (lines 145, 153) which would throw next if budgets were defined. The fix from iter4 was only half-applied.

integration_issues.issue: Dashboard calls api.analytics, api.listTransactions, api.getSavings in load() but does NOT call api.getBudgets(), api.getStreaks(), api.getSettings(). All three endpoints already exist on the api client (see /app/frontend/src/api.js lines 50/53/54) and backend is green. Wiring is the only missing piece.

react_native_issues.issue: Render-time ReferenceError takes over the entire authenticated app — no other tab is reachable because routing pushes user to /(tabs) (Dashboard) right after login.

action_items: After adding the state, verify Dashboard mounts cleanly, then re-run frontend tests: streak cards visible, set weekly budget ₹100 + add ₹200 expense → warning-danger banner with title 'You exceeded your weekly budget'; toggle profile discipline-toggle ON → return to dashboard → title becomes 'Budget exceeded. Streak lost.' and hero card has heroDanger reddish border.
critical_code_review_comments: index.tsx line 48-56 references runtime symbols `budgets`, `settings`, `streaks` that are never declared or fetched. Compile passes (JS/TS only catches lexical scope; these are not declared at all so they fall through to global). RUNTIME CRASH on every mount.
budget.tsx — period-aware label fix at line 81 looks correct: `Spent this {period}`. Untested live because Dashboard blocks navigation.
Recommend adding a top-level error boundary so a bad render in a tab doesn't black-hole the whole app.
Consider try/catch around individual Promise.all members so a single failing endpoint (eg /api/streaks) doesn't blank the dashboard once wired.

success_rate.frontend: 0% — Dashboard crashes on mount, same as iter4

seed_data_creation: None new this iteration; previous iter4 ephemeral users still exist.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Reproduction: GET https://smooth-spend.preview.emergentagent.com → onboarding → /login → fill test@nsiap.com / test1234 → click Sign In → red screen 'budgets is not defined' at index.tsx:48. After E1 wires up the three state hooks + adds the three API calls to load(), retest: (a) Dashboard renders, no red screen; (b) streak-log and streak-budget testIDs visible; (c) navigate Budget → tap budget-period-week → set ₹100 → add ₹200 Food expense via FAB → back to Dashboard → expect testID warning-danger with title 'You exceeded your weekly budget'; (d) toggle Profile discipline-toggle ON → back to Dashboard → title becomes 'Budget exceeded. Streak lost.', heroDanger border visible; (e) Budget tab — verify 'Spent this week' label appears under Weekly tab.
rca_of_issue: RCA — The fix shipped in iter5 was incomplete: only the compute block was inserted (lines 41-76 of index.tsx). The compute block consumes three identifiers (`budgets`, `settings`, `streaks`) that have no declaration in the file. There is no useState, no destructure from a hook, and no import binding for them. JS resolves them as undeclared globals, which throws ReferenceError at first render. Mitigation = declare three state variables and extend the existing Promise.all in load() with api.getBudgets()/getStreaks()/getSettings(). All three endpoints exist on the api client and the backend already returns 200 for them (verified iter4 17/17 pytest).
